title: Skeleton Rigging Joint Hierarchy Viewer
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js (via CDN) that implements an interactive skeleton rigging joint hierarchy viewer.

## Scene Setup
- Dark background (0x1a1a2e) with a subtle grid floor (20x20, color 0x333355) at y=0.
- Perspective camera at position (0, 5, 12), looking at origin.
- Ambient light (0x404060, intensity 0.4) and a directional light (0xffffff, intensity 0.8) from (5, 10, 7).
- OrbitControls for camera manipulation.

## Skeleton Hierarchy
- Build a humanoid skeleton hierarchy using Three.js Bone objects with the following structure:
  - Root (hip) at (0, 4, 0)
    - Spine at relative (0, 1.2, 0)
      - Chest at relative (0, 1.2, 0)
        - Head at relative (0, 1.0, 0)
        - LeftShoulder at relative (-0.8, 0.8, 0)
          - LeftElbow at relative (-1.2, 0, 0)
            - LeftHand at relative (-1.0, 0, 0)
        - RightShoulder at relative (0.8, 0.8, 0)
          - RightElbow at relative (1.2, 0, 0)
            - RightHand at relative (1.0, 0, 0)
    - LeftHip at relative (-0.5, -0.2, 0)
      - LeftKnee at relative (0, -1.8, 0)
        - LeftFoot at relative (0, -1.8, 0)
    - RightHip at relative (0.5, -0.2, 0)
      - RightKnee at relative (0, -1.8, 0)
        - RightFoot at relative (0, -1.8, 0)
- Create a Three.js Skeleton from these bones.
- Visualize each joint as a small sphere (radius 0.15, 0x00ffaa emissive material).
- Connect parent-child joints with cylinder or line segments (color 0x4488ff, linewidth or thin cylinders).

## Selection & Interaction
- Clicking on a joint sphere selects it. The selected joint turns yellow (0xffff00). Only one joint selected at a time.
- When a joint is selected, display its name, world position (x,y,z), local rotation (Euler degrees), and depth in hierarchy in an info panel (top-right DOM overlay, styled dark semi-transparent box).
- Keyboard controls:
  - 'R' key: rotate the selected joint +15 degrees around its local Z-axis. All child joints must follow (FK chain).
  - 'E' key: rotate the selected joint -15 degrees around its local Z-axis (reverse).
  - 'X' key: rotate the selected joint +15 degrees around its local X-axis.
  - 'T' key: toggle visibility of all bone connection lines on/off.
  - 'H' key: toggle highlight mode — when ON, hovering over a joint highlights it and all its descendants in orange (0xff8800). When OFF, no hover effect.
  - 'A' key: play a simple idle animation that oscillates the spine bone rotation on Z between -10 and +10 degrees over 2 seconds, looping. Press 'A' again to stop.
  - 'Space' key: reset all joint rotations to their initial default (zero local rotation).

## Hierarchy Tree Panel
- On the left side, render a DOM-based collapsible tree view showing the bone hierarchy with indentation.
- Each tree node shows the bone name. Clicking a tree node selects that joint (same as clicking the 3D sphere).
- The currently selected joint's tree node should be highlighted with a colored background.

## State Management
- Expose window.__3D_STATE__ with:
  - jointCount: total number of joints (should be 15)
  - selectedJoint: name string of currently selected joint, or null
  - selectedJointDepth: integer depth of selected joint in hierarchy (root=0)
  - selectedJointWorldPos: {x, y, z} world position of selected joint
  - selectedJointLocalRotZ: local Z rotation of selected joint in degrees
  - selectedJointLocalRotX: local X rotation of selected joint in degrees
  - bonesVisible: boolean, whether bone connection lines are visible
  - highlightMode: boolean, whether highlight mode is on
  - animationPlaying: boolean, whether idle animation is active
  - spineRotationZ: current spine bone local Z rotation in degrees
  - totalRotationsApplied: integer counter incremented each time R, E, or X is pressed on a selected joint
  - hierarchyTreeNodeCount: number of DOM tree nodes rendered
  - childCountOfSelected: number of direct children of the selected joint
  - resetCount: integer counter incremented each time Space resets rotations

## HUD
- Top-left: display 'Joints: 15 | Selected: [name]' updating in real time.
- Bottom: small instruction bar listing key bindings.

Ensure all state updates happen synchronously with user actions. The skeleton must use proper Three.js Bone parenting so FK propagation is automatic.
physics_constraints: Forward kinematics must propagate correctly: rotating a parent joint must move all descendant joints accordingly. World positions of child joints must change when parent is rotated. Resetting rotations must restore all joints to initial world positions within epsilon 0.01.
